'''Jamie Bloem''' (born 26 May 1971) is a former dual code rugby player and rugby league referee. He currently coaches amateur rugby league side [[Elland ARLFC]]. He played representative rugby league for [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]] and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]. At club level for [[Cape Town Coasters]] (South Africa), [[Castleford Tigers]],<ref>David Smart & Andrew Howard (1 July 2000) "Images of Sport – Castleford Rugby League – A Twentieth Century History". The History Press Ltd. {{ISBN|978-0752418957}}</ref><ref name="Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|title=Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084010/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|archivedate=16 February 2012}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=265|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|url-status=unfit|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084416/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=265|archivedate=16 February 2012}}</ref> [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]],<ref name="Statistics at robterrace.com">{{cite web|url=http://robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=670|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180224232639/http://robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=670|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 February 2018|title=Statistics at robterrace.com|publisher=robterrace.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> [[Widnes Vikings]],<ref name="Rugby League: Widnes sign South African Jamie Bloem">{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-widnes-sign-south-african-jamie-bloem-1349741.html|title=Rugby League: Widnes sign South African Jamie Bloem|work=The Independent|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>[[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Panthers]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[player-coach]] for [[Stainland#Sport|Stainland Stags ARLFC]], as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce|sh|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/jamie-bloem/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite book|last1=Hardcastle|first1=Andrew|title=In Full Bloem: Jamie Bloem – Rugby Footballer|date=2012|publisher=London League Publications|isbn=978-1903659656}}</ref>

In rugby union he played for [[Western Province (rugby team)|Western Province]] at representative level, and at club level for [[Union Milnerton RFC]] ([[Cape Town]]), [[Racing 92|Racing Métro 92]], [[Halifax RUFC]] and [[player-coach]] for [[Thornensians RUFC]], [[Old Brodleians]] RUFC, [[Old Rishworthians]] RUFC, and Halifax Vandals RUFC.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="official.sportnetwork.net">{{cite web|url=http://official.sportnetwork.net/main/s347/st61143.htm|title=official.sportnetwork.net|publisher=official.sportnetwork.net|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="South Africa Shown Up By Tonga">{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/south-africa-shown-up-by-tonga-636078.html|title=South Africa Shown Up By Tonga|work=The Independent|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Rugby league==
===Club career===
Bloem initially played his club rugby league for the Cape Town Coasters in [[Rugby league in South Africa|South Africa]],<ref name="Reports 2003 at sportinglife.com">{{cite web|url=http://sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/reports2003/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/03/08/29/RUGBYL_Castleford_Nightlead.html&TEAMHD=castleford&HEADER=&YEAR=2003|title=Reports 2003 at sportinglife.com|work=Sporting Life|location=UK|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018|archive-date=16 July 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110716023116/https://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/reports2003/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F03%2F08%2F29%2FRUGBYL_Castleford_Nightlead.html&TEAMHD=castleford&HEADER=&YEAR=2003|url-status=dead}}</ref> he also played in France and has travelled extensively as a player, he became [[player-coach]] at [[Stainland#Sport|Stainland Stags ARLFC]] ([[Halifax, West Yorkshire]]) during the [[Pennine League]] 2010–11 season.

===Drugs ban===
In November 1994, Bloem was the first rugby league player to test positive for performance enhancing anabolic steroid [[nandrolone]], he was banned for two years.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Mottram|first1=David R.|title=Drugs in sport|date=1996|publisher=E. & F.N. Spon|isbn=0-419-18890-8}}</ref><ref name="Rugby League – League To Step Up Drug Testing">{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league--league-to-step-up-drug-testing-1569714.html|title=Rugby League – League To Step Up Drug Testing|work=The Independent|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Bloem was the [[Captain (sports)|captain]] of [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]] (RL) during the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]],<ref name="South Africa Shown Up By Tonga"/><ref name="World Cup 2000 – Group 2">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/world_cup_2000/group_2/978377.stm|title=World Cup 2000 – Group 2|publisher=BBC|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> he was eligible for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] (RL) through his mother's nationality.<ref name="official.sportnetwork.net"/>

==Rugby union==
Bloem has also had spells with rugby union sides; [[Halifax RUFC]], [[Thornensians RUFC]] ([[Thorne, South Yorkshire|Thorne]], [[Doncaster]]), and [[Old Brodleians]] RUFC ([[Hipperholme]], [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]]), whom he took to their first promotion in 75 years, he transferred to [[Old Rishworthians]] RUFC ([[Rishworth]], [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]]) as [[player-coach]] for the 2007–08 season, he was the top point scorer for Old Rishworthians in 2007–08, breaking the club record with 375 points in the season, he became [[player-coach]] at Halifax Vandals RUFC at the start of the 2013–2014 season.

==Later years==
Bloem became a BBC radio rugby league sports summariser,<ref name="BBC radio rugby league sports summariser">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/solpda/ifs_sport/hi/newsid_8027000/8027754.stm|title=BBC radio rugby league sports summariser|publisher=BBC|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}{{Dead link|date=February 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> and a rugby league referee and controlled the Nordic Cup match between [[Norway national rugby league team|Norway]] and [[Sweden national rugby league team|Sweden]] at [[Partille]] Ground ([[Spartacus Rugby Club]]), [[Gothenburg]] on Saturday 30 October 2010.<ref name="Bloem looks forward to officiating Nordic Cup at rleague.com">{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/db/article.php?id=37969|title=Bloem looks forward to officiating Nordic Cup at rleague.com|publisher=rleague.com|date=28 October 2010|accessdate=1 January 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101115183922/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=37969|archive-date=15 November 2010|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Bloem looks forward to officiating Nordic Cup at rlef.eu.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1493|title=Bloem looks forward to officiating Nordic Cup at rlef.eu.com|publisher=rlef.eu.com|date=28 October 2010|accessdate=1 January 2011|archive-date=6 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180206002620/http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1493|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Referee Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/referees/jamie-bloem.html|title=Referee Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Bloem was born in [[Melbourne]], [[Victoria (Australia)|Victoria]], Australia to Scottish and South African parents, his twin sister died at the age of six weeks. He was brought up in apartheid-torn South Africa on a military base where his father was stationed. Bloems father died in action when he was 15.

After retiring from rugby Bloem set up a [[Landscape architecture|landscape gardening]] business in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]].<ref name="Bloem Close To Crossing Divide">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/12/03/13/RUGBYL_Column.html&BID=480|title=Bloem Close To Crossing Divide|work=Sporting Life|location=UK|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}{{Dead link|date=September 2024 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Bloem was wrongly accused of having sex with a minor, the charge was found to be completely without foundation.<ref name="Former Halifax Rugby League star Jamie Bloem speaks of hell after being accused of child sex offence">{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/news/crime/former-halifax-rugby-league-star-jamie-bloem-speaks-of-hell-after-being-accused-of-child-sex-offence-1-5053146|title=Former Halifax Rugby League star Jamie Bloem speaks of hell after being accused of child sex offence|publisher=halifaxcourier.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018|archive-date=6 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180206131458/https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/news/crime/former-halifax-rugby-league-star-jamie-bloem-speaks-of-hell-after-being-accused-of-child-sex-offence-1-5053146|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Hard-hitting Bloem paid a heavy price for playing the game he loved">{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/lifestyle/nostalgia/hard-hitting-bloem-paid-a-heavy-price-for-playing-the-game-he-loved-1-5501568|title=Hard-hitting Bloem paid a heavy price for playing the game he loved|publisher=halifaxcourier.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018|archive-date=6 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180206131611/https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/lifestyle/nostalgia/hard-hitting-bloem-paid-a-heavy-price-for-playing-the-game-he-loved-1-5501568|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Innocent former Huddersfield Giants star Jamie Bloem speaks about the nightmare allegation of child sex offence">{{cite web|url=https://www.examiner.co.uk/news/west-yorkshire-news/innocent-former-huddersfield-giants-star-4941682|title=Innocent former Huddersfield Giants star Jamie Bloem speaks about the nightmare allegation of child sex offence|publisher=examiner.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>
